name: Golf Envy South Tempe
address: 7420 S Rural Rd Suite B2-3, Tempe, AZ 85284
about: Golf Envy South Tempe is a private indoor golf membership club equipped with cutting-edge simulators. The facility brings world-renowned golf courses, including Pebble Beach and St. Andrews, to an indoor, climate-controlled environment. Members can play and practice year-round on hundreds of actual courses with life-like playing conditions, accessible 24 hours a day through an app-based reservation system. The venue emphasizes a country club feel, offering analytics for game improvement, social events, and the option to host private gatherings.
why_golfers_like_it: Access to hundreds of world-class courses like Pebble Beach and St. Andrews in a single facility
Advanced swing analytics including spin rate, loft angle, and flight path for game improvement
24/7 climate-controlled environment for practice regardless of weather conditions
Exclusive, country club-like atmosphere for networking with fellow golf enthusiasts

food_and_drink.notes: No mention of on-site food service
No mention of on-site alcohol service

simulator_hardware.notes: Advanced simulator with upgraded Motion Plate, powered by Unreal Engine 5.x

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: The text does not specify, but as a golf simulator venue, it is standard practice to allow members to use their own clubs.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: The website does not mention any on-site food or beverage service, suggesting a BYOB (Bring Your Own Beverage) policy, but this is not explicitly confirmed.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: The venue highlights the ability to play hundreds of actual courses, including famed Pebble Beach, all in under an hour, suggesting a rapid play experience typical of high-end simulators.

faq.question: Is it kid-friendly?
faq.answer: The venue is described as a private indoor golf membership club with an adult-oriented country club feel, and there is no mention of youth programs or family events, indicating it is likely not kid-friendly.
